summary: SSO Routes Refactor and UI Enhancements tested successfully. Backend: 11/11 tests passed (Auth0/Okta status, login, providers, discover, regular auth). Frontend: All UI elements verified - Marketplace with notary cards/filters/search, Notary profile with Write Review button and interactive star rating (1-5), Organization invite modal with Admin/Member role cards with descriptions and icons.

issues: Availability section not visible for John Notary - expected behavior when notary has no availability data configured

critical_code_review_comments: SSO routes refactor successful - sso_routes.py split into sso_common.py, auth0_routes.py, okta_routes.py, and sso_routes.py
All SSO endpoints maintain same URL paths after refactor (/api/sso/auth0/*, /api/sso/okta/*, /api/sso/*)
Auth0 and Okta login endpoints return HTTP 200 with valid auth_url (Okta was previously returning 400)
Marketplace UI has proper data-testid attributes for testing
Review form has interactive star rating with proper state management
Organization invite modal has enhanced role selection with descriptions and icons
seed_data_creation: None - used existing users and notaries
context_for_next_testing_agent: SSO routes refactor verified working. Auth0 and Okta endpoints at /api/sso/auth0/* and /api/sso/okta/* respectively. Marketplace notary profile detail shows Write Review button when logged in - clicking opens review form with interactive 5-star rating. Organization invite modal at /organizations shows Admin/Member role cards with descriptions. Availability section only shows when notary has availability data.

POST /api/auth/login.note: Regular login still works after refactor

availability_preview.note: Only shows when notary has availability data

role_descriptions.admin: Full access — manage members, billing, settings, and SSO
role_descriptions.member: Standard access — create and manage own documents

organization_invite_modal.role_icons: Shield for Admin, Users for Member

login_page_sso_buttons.auth0_redirect: Successfully redirects to Auth0 login page
